items.title: Ook De Busser gelinkt aan Feyenoord: 'Ik heb nog niks gehoord'
items.summary: Mats Deijl gaat Go Ahead Eagles hoogstwaarschijnlijk verruilen voor Feyenoord. Mogelijk krijgt de aanvoerder van de Deventenaren gezelschap van Jari De Busser. De Rotterdammers houden de Belg in de gaten nu Justin Bijlow is vertrokken naar Genoa. De keeper hoorde vooralsnog niets.

items.title: Man City players to refund fans after Bodø loss
items.summary: Manchester City's captains have announced they will refund the cost of tickets for nearly 400 supporters.

items.title: Hayes: Returning Rodman ready for 'next steps'
items.summary: USWNT coach Emma Hayes said Trinity Rodman is ready to shoulder more responsibility and take the "next steps" in her career as she returns to the national team.

items.title: France won't boycott World Cup over Greenland
items.summary: France's sports minister said her country is not currently thinking about boycotting the FIFA World Cup in the United States amid growing tensions related to Donald Trump's quest to control Greenland.

items.title: Bellingham on drinking celeb: 'I know the truth'
items.summary: Jude Bellingham described his drinking gesture goal celebration as "a bit of a joke" aimed at speculation about his off-the-field behaviour.

items.title: Cobden to miss Cheltenham rides through shoulder injury
items.summary: Harry Cobden will miss Cheltenham’s meeting on Saturday having failed to sufficiently recover in time from an injury picked up in a fall at Ascot last weekend.

items.title: Juve verliest rechtszaak van Ronaldo: tien miljoen euro blijft bij Portugees
items.summary: Juventus heeft een rechtszaak verloren van Cristiano Ronaldo, voormalig speler van de club. De rechter heeft bepaald dat de Italiaanse club niet het recht had om in 2024 bijna tien miljoen euro terug te eisen van de Portugese superster.

items.title: Jans blijft strijdbaar met FC Utrecht: 'Anders had ik hier niet gezeten'
items.summary: Ron Jans kent een zwakke fase met FC Utrecht. De hoofdtrainer behoudt het vertrouwen dat hij de negatieve spiraal kan doorbreken. Aan opstappen heeft hij nooit gedacht. 'Er zijn weleens dingen door mijn hoofd gegaan, maar ik heb nog nooit een trainersbaan opgegeven en ben ook niet van plan om dat nu te doen.'

items.title: 'No desire' for World Cup boycott over Greenland
items.summary: The French government is not currently in favour of boycotting this summer's World Cup over US President Donald Trump's threats to annex Greenland, according to the country's sports minister.

items.title: Van Persie verklaart eigen aanpak en gaat in op terugkerend thema bij Feyenoord
items.summary: Robin van Persie kreeg op de persconferentie richting het Europa League-duel van Feyenoord met Sturm Graz vragen over zijn werkwijze als hoofdtrainer en de beeldvorming van de buitenwacht. Hij gaf tekst en uitleg. 'Als speler was ik ook nooit bezig met wat anderen van mij vonden.'

items.title: Senegalese president dankt Afrika Cup-winnaars met geldbonus én stuk land
items.summary: Naast de eeuwige roem die het winnen van de Afrika Cup de spelers van Senegal heeft opgeleverd, legt de prestatie de selectie sowieso geen windeieren. Ook de president is de spelers zeer erkentelijk en dus beloont Bassirou Diomaye Faye de voetballers ruim.

items.title: Frank earns breathing space after rolling Tottenham dice – could Europe shield him from sack?
items.summary: Manager showed tactical bravery in darkest hour and like Postecoglou may find respite outside Premier League Thomas Frank was backed into a corner on every level, including team selection. The Tottenham manager had next-to-no options, only 11 established outfield players for the Champions League home game against Borussia Dortmund on Tuesday night. The expectation was for an either/or choice between Djed Spence and Destiny Udogie at left-back. But when the teamsheets dropped, it turned out Frank had picked them both and left Randal Kolo Muani on the bench. It was the latest low point for Kolo Muani, who has to be regretting his decision to join on a season-long loan from Paris Saint-Germain. Continue reading...

items.title: Vinícius Júnior's future at Real Madrid: Stay, exit or run contract down?
items.summary: Vinícius has returned to form for Real Madrid, but what should he do about his future with the club? Here are his options.

items.title: Brentford latest: Two matches live on Sky in March
Spurs, Arsenal or Newcastle - which teams have had most injuries?
items.summary: BBC Sport crunches the numbers to assess which Premier League teams have been hit hardest by injuries this season.

items.title: Ireland include two uncapped players in experienced Six Nations squad
items.summary: Ireland have named uncapped duo Nathan Doak and Edwin Edogbo in an otherwise experienced Six Nations squad.

items.title: Man City squad to refund ticket costs for fans
items.summary: Manchester City's squad will refund the ticket costs for 374 supporters who travelled to Norway to witness their side's shock 3-1 Champions League defeat at Bodo/Glimt on Tuesday.

items.title: Crawley returns as opener for first England ODI against Sri Lanka
As their midwinter slump goes on, what exactly is going wrong at Manchester City? | Jamie Jackson
items.summary: Manchester City have issues with injury and form, and need their big players to step up and turn the ship around At Bodø/Glimt, in a first Champions League outing since 1 October, the 29-year-old appeared what he is: a player still recovering after 18 months out with a serious knee injury and several related setbacks. This was only a third start since his latest return began with the second 45 minutes of the goalless draw at Sunderland on New Year’s Day. Last week Rodri declared he was “ready to go” and said: “I’m really happy to be on the pitch every single day.” Yet in Saturday’s 2-0 loss at Manchester United he was a one-paced, non-factor unable to do what he did with ease pre‑anterior cruciate ligament rupture: run midfield and so the contest. In Tuesday’s 3-1 humbling in Norway the Spaniard was the same, and two moments tell the tale of his form. First Jens Petter Hauge left him a statue before registering a memorable long-range strike for Bodø’s third goal; then came the two yellow cards in two minutes that had Rodri sent off. Continue reading...

items.title: Palace striker Mateta tells club he wants to leave
items.summary: France striker Jean-Philippe Mateta has 18 months remaining on his current deal at Selhurst Park and as things stand he will not be signing an extension.
